name: Aslaug
description: Aslaug, also known as Kráka, is a figure of considerable intrigue in Norse mythology. She is primarily known as the wife of the legendary Viking Ragnar Lothbrok and the mother of his famous sons, including Bjorn Ironside, Ivar the Boneless, Hvitserk, and Sigurd Snake-in-the-Eye. Her lineage is often traced back to Sigurd the dragon slayer and the Valkyrie Brynhildr, adding a layer of heroic and divine ancestry to her character. Accounts of Aslaug vary, but she is consistently portrayed as a woman of exceptional beauty, intelligence, and prophetic abilities. Her tale is often intertwined with themes of fate, destiny, and the consequences of broken promises. She possessed seer-like qualities, capable of foretelling future events, and her wisdom was highly valued. The sagas frequently emphasize her cunning and strategic mind, suggesting her role extended beyond that of a mere wife and mother to one of a trusted advisor.
subtitle: Wise Queen of the North

keyMyths.title: The Serpent's Eye
keyMyths.description: Aslaug prophesied that her son Sigurd would be born with a serpent in his eye if Ragnar waited three nights before consummating their marriage. Ragnar, arrogant and eager to prove his self-control, defied her warning. Sigurd was indeed born with a mark in his eye, a symbol of his destiny and lineage. This myth highlights Aslaug's prophetic abilities and the consequences of ignoring her wisdom, ultimately impacting the fate of her son and his future deeds.

keyMyths.title: Ragnar's Wooing
keyMyths.description: Before Ragnar knew of Aslaug's true identity and lineage, he was captivated by her beauty and wisdom. He sent his men to test her intelligence by asking her to arrive 'neither dressed nor undressed, neither hungry nor full, and neither alone nor in company.' Aslaug cleverly fulfilled these impossible demands by arriving wrapped in a fishing net, biting an onion, and accompanied by a dog, proving her wit and securing Ragnar's admiration and eventual marriage proposal.

keyMyths.title: The Lineage of Sigurd and Brynhildr
keyMyths.description: Aslaug's claim to be the daughter of Sigurd and Brynhildr is a central element of her identity. This lineage connects her to the heroic age of Norse mythology, imbuing her with a sense of destiny and importance. While the exact details of her parentage are debated across different sagas, the connection to these legendary figures solidifies her position as a woman of exceptional birth and foreshadows the heroic deeds of her sons, who inherit the legacy of their ancestors.
keyMyths.source: Volsunga Saga and The Saga of Ragnar Lothbrok

sources.author: Crawford, Jackson
sources.work: The Saga of the Volsungs: With the Saga of Ragnar Lothbrok
sources.description: A modern translation and compilation of the Volsunga Saga and the Saga of Ragnar Lothbrok, providing key narratives about Aslaug's life and lineage.
sources.text: A modern translation and compilation of the Volsunga Saga and the Saga of Ragnar Lothbrok, providing key narratives about Aslaug's life and lineage.

sources.author: Byock, Jesse L.
sources.work: The Saga of King Hrolf Kraki
sources.description: This saga provides additional context for the historical and mythological landscape in which Aslaug's story unfolds, illuminating the cultural values and beliefs of the time.
sources.text: This saga provides additional context for the historical and mythological landscape in which Aslaug's story unfolds, illuminating the cultural values and beliefs of the time.

sources.author: Davidson, H.R. Ellis
sources.work: Gods and Myths of Northern Europe
sources.description: A comprehensive overview of Norse mythology, providing background information on the deities, heroes, and legends that shape Aslaug's story.
sources.text: A comprehensive overview of Norse mythology, providing background information on the deities, heroes, and legends that shape Aslaug's story.
